title: harre.dev
description: harre.dev - A collection of thoughts and ideas

items.title: Tactical versus strategic coding
items.content_html:
Tactical versus strategic coding

Vacation time for me generally means reading time and for this occasion I brought "A philosophy of software design" by John Ousterhout with me. So over the course of a few warm summer evenings I read my way through it and here are some thoughts and an interesting thing I picked up.

items.title: Dotnet Core Entity Framework Migrations on Cloud Foundry
items.content_html:
Dotnet Core Entity Framework Migrations on Cloud Foundry

Run database migrations on Cloud Foundry using a task to ensure that migrations are only done once on deployment (and not during app start up!) for dotnet core applications. This post assumes you are deploying built applications and not raw source code.

items.title: 6 Months with the Fairphone 5
items.content_html:
6 Months in with the Fairphone 5

Somewhere in December of 2023 I got my Fairphone 5 delivered. I was using an iPhone 12 mini at the time that was suffering from a pretty degraded battery and was in need of something else. That iPhone was my first one and also my last, the lack of customization on iOS was also getting a bit of an annoyance so the choice for Android was not a difficult one.

items.title: EV Driving
items.content_html:
EV Driving

Having done a long drive into France not that long ago using an EV (a 2020 Polestar 2), I encountered some stuff that I thought was worth writing down.

items.title: Polestar 2 battery state API
items.content_html:
Polestar 2 battery state API

Update in 2024:

It appears that Polestar is working on a proper API and folks have been figuring out how to get to the data used by the mobile app. See here for an example of a custom Home Assistant integration that surfaces quite a bit more than just the state of charge. This code will still work even with the existence of a polestar API but be aware that an official API might at some point appear.

Polestar (the car manufaturer) offers an app to control (a very limited) set of features of their cars remoteley (locks, and climate) and can display the state of the battery in % remaining. It's nice to have but the app isn't all that great and sometimes takes very long to show up-to-date information.

items.title: HVC Trash pickup dates in Home Assistant
items.content_html:
HVC Trash pickup dates in Home Assistant

Maybe something you can use, maybe not, but if you are in an intersection of people who use Home Assistant and live in the Netherlands and in a region where HVC operates, this might be useful.

items.title: Gaming on your phone
items.content_html:
Mobile gaming

Time for something else for a change. Games! Summer vacation is here and you're going to be away from your computer for a while. You still want to play some games, but you don't want to lug around your laptop.

items.title: Self-host FreshRSS on a Microsoft Azure Web App
items.content_html:
Self-host FreshRSS on a Microsoft Azure Web App

I'm getting back to the days of old with RSS and since I really don't want to depend on any third party service the only way forward is self hosting.

items.title: Valheim public-test server
items.content_html:
Valheim public-test server

UPDATE

The testing appears to be done and is now part of the regular version of the game. This is still a handy guide for any future betas though.

Here's a cool update for your Valheim linux server if you're up from some beta testing.

items.title: Today I learned - kubectl replace
items.content_html:
Today I learned - kubectl replace

Here's one for your toolbox, but use with care. Say you have an app running on kubernetes in a pod and you spot something you want to change. This is one way to get it done.

items.title: It all starts with smaller stories
items.content_html:
It all starts with smaller stories

I think most developers have experienced a taks sitting in progress for days or even weeks (or... months!) on end. This long running work eventually gets merged and released but it turns out it's the wrong thing and a large part needs rework.

items.title: My first podcast appearance
items.content_html:
My first podcast appearance (in Dutch)

On the 9th of August 2022 Robert Kloosterhuis and myself recorded a podcast episode with the nice folks at CodeCapi. They invited us over to talk about developer experience. A pretty broad topic and we covered a lot of stuff in the recording.

items.title: Paved road - Templates
items.content_html:
Paved road - Application Templates

In my previous post about paving the road to production there were some ideas around offering deployment pipelines as a service in your organization. In this post, we're going to take it up a level and have a look what we can do to remove even more friction, specifically when creating a new app from scratch.

items.title: Paved road - Deployments
items.content_html:
Paved road - Deployments

Speeding up developer teams by eliminating waste and busywork can come in many forms. A huge step comes through automation, deployment specifically. Getting your app to production in an automated manner through some sort of pipeline is a huge timesaver.

items.title: Valheim server on Linux
items.content_html:
Valheim server on Linux

Here is a step-by-step guide for setting up a Valheim server on Linux. Having done it twice now I thought it would be a good idea to capture this somewhere so that next time I don't have to look any of this up again.

items.title: Microsoft Edge Picture-in-Picture
items.content_html:
Microsoft Edge Picture-in-Picture

Small update: Edge now supports picture-in-picture with a little button out of the box! So you no longer need the code snippet.

Here's a quick little helper to pop out videoplayers in Microsoft Edge.

items.title: It's new tech!
items.content_html:
It's new tech!

I finally came up on the best excuse to upgrade my computer. My very generous employer, ITQ, gave us the opportunity to upgrade our home office and that brought me the base for a new system.

items.title: Dotnet core 5 on a raspberry pi
items.content_html:
dotnet core 5 on a raspberry pi

Here's a quick get-up-and-go style post on how to get dotnet core 5 usable on a Raspberry Pi (a R-Pi 3 Model B in my case).

items.title: The great quarantine of 2020
items.content_html:
The great quarantine of 2020

We're almost at the end of 2020 and we're not really quarantining anymore but kinda still are.

items.title: Some fun with Pico-8
items.content_html:
Some fun with Pico-8

I had a some tine left in the day (who doesn't, with all the quarantine and all...) so why not spend that time programming.
